controlled_terms.definition: An HTTP method and path pair in the Petstore API contract.
The reusable Petstore data shape referenced by operations or responses.

items.statement: The compiled authority should preserve the Petstore API's endpoint, parameter, response, and schema contracts closely enough for downstream API planning agents.
items.title: API contract fidelity

items.statement: The benchmark fixture is not intended to model the full Swagger Petstore surface beyond the listed paths, parameters, responses, and schemas.
items.title: Full OpenAPI surface

acceptance: The API contract includes a GET operation at /pets.
The GET /pets success response is 200 and returns the Pets schema.
The GET /pets default response returns the Error schema.
items.statement: The API must expose GET /pets to list pets and must define both the expected success response returning Pets and the default error response returning Error.
items.title: List pets operation

acceptance: The API contract includes a POST operation at /pets.
The POST /pets operation accepts a Pet request body.
The POST /pets success response is 201 and returns the created Pet schema.
The POST /pets default response returns the Error schema.
items.statement: The API must expose POST /pets to create a pet from a Pet request body and must define both the expected created-pet success response and the default error response returning Error.
items.title: Create pet operation

acceptance: The API contract includes a GET operation at /pets/{petId}.
The GET /pets/{petId} success response is 200 and returns the Pet schema.
The GET /pets/{petId} default response returns the Error schema.
items.statement: The API must expose GET /pets/{petId} to fetch a single pet and must define both the expected success response returning Pet and the default error response returning Error.
items.title: Get pet operation

acceptance: The GET /pets operation defines a limit query parameter.
The limit query parameter maximum value is 100.
items.statement: The GET /pets limit query parameter must have a maximum value of 100.
items.title: Limit query maximum

acceptance: The GET /pets/{petId} operation defines petId as a path parameter.
The petId path parameter is required.
items.statement: The GET /pets/{petId} operation must define petId as a required path parameter.
items.title: Required petId path parameter

acceptance: The Pet schema defines an id field.
The Pet schema defines a name field.
The Pet schema marks id and name as required.
items.statement: The Pet schema must require the id and name fields.
items.title: Pet schema required fields

acceptance: The Pets schema is an array.
The Pets schema items are Pet objects.
The Pets schema maxItems value is 100.
items.statement: The Pets schema must be an array of Pet objects with maxItems equal to 100.
items.title: Pets array schema

acceptance: The Error schema defines a code field.
The Error schema defines a message field.
The Error schema marks code and message as required.
items.statement: The Error schema must require the code and message fields.
items.title: Error schema required fields

problem_statement: A compact Petstore API contract must compile into authority that preserves endpoint, parameter, response, and schema semantics without depending on prose extraction.

relations.rationale: The list operation advances the API contract fidelity goal.
The create operation advances the API contract fidelity goal.
The get operation advances the API contract fidelity goal.
The maximum constrains the list operation's limit parameter.
The required path parameter constrains the get-pet operation.
The list success response depends on the Pets schema.
The create operation request and success response depend on the Pet schema.
The get success response depends on the Pet schema.
The default error response depends on the Error schema.
The default error response depends on the Error schema.
The default error response depends on the Error schema.

summary: Defines the compact Petstore API contract used by the authority-quality benchmark.
title: Petstore API Technical Spec
